# CAMELS Analysis: Liquidity & Funding (L) – Comerica Incorporated, 2020

**Assessment: Adequate**

---

## Key Metrics & Regulatory Context

Comerica's liquidity position in 2020 reflects a materially improved regulatory environment following the Economic Growth, Regulatory Relief and Consumer Protection Act (EGRRCPA) of May 2018. As a bank holding company with consolidated assets below $100 billion, Comerica became exempt from enhanced prudential standards applicable to larger institutions [Source: p.38]. # CAMELS Analysis: Capital One Financial Corporation (2023)

**Assessment: Adequate**

---

## Key Metrics & Income Statement Performance

Capital One demonstrated solid revenue growth in 2023, with total net revenue reaching $36,787 million, representing a 7% increase year-over-year from $34,250 million in 2022 [Source: p.124]. This growth was underpinned by robust interest income expansion of 34% to $41,938 million, up from $31,237 million in 2022, reflecting the benefit of a higher interest rate environment [Source: p.124]. # CAMELS ANALYSIS: REGIONS FINANCIAL CORPORATION – 2020

**Assessment: Adequate**

---

## Key Metrics & Regulatory Classification

Regions Financial operates as a Category IV banking organization under the Federal Reserve's Tailoring Rules finalized in October 2019, reflecting its status as a bank holding company with total consolidated assets exceeding $100 billion [Source: p.52]. This classification subjects the institution to enhanced prudential standards while exempting it from certain stringent requirements applicable to larger systemically important banks. As loan quality deteriorates, the problem loan ratio rises, signaling potential problems such as credit losses and consequent pressure

The numerator of the ratio is total problem loans. We define problem loans as (i) "impaired loans," as defined for finance companies

Static discounted cash flow models are associated with deterministic models. In deterministic 
models, the user designates an interest rate scenario, and the model generates an exposure 
estimate for the scenario. Stochastic models use rate scenarios that are randomly generated.

can be calculated from the distribution of estimates (common forms of stochastic models are 
Monte Carlo and binomial interest rate trees).
